title: Podman has terribly slow internet connection
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Clone https://github.com/DavidBurela/riscv-emulator-docker-image
2. Run `podman build .` inside it
3. Everything from apt to git takes eternity to finish, Git does not finish and there are missing bytes always


### Describe the results you received

Everything from apt to Git take eternity to finish. Git fails with missing bytes:
```
STEP 5/8: RUN git clone https://github.com/qemu/qemu && mkdir /root/qemu/build  && cd /root/qemu/build && ../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu && make -j3 && make install && rm /root/qemu -r
Cloning into 'qemu'...
error: 780 bytes of body are still expected
fetch-pack: unexpected disconnect while reading sideband packet
fatal: early EOF
fatal: fetch-pack: invalid index-pack output
Error: building at STEP "RUN git clone https://github.com/qemu/qemu && mkdir /root/qemu/build  && cd /root/qemu/build && ../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu && make -j3 && make install && rm /root/qemu -r": while running runtime: exit status 128
```

### Describe the results you expected

Successful build of image. 

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Do you run as root or rootless? Does it work better in a container with `podman run`? Have you tried `--network=host`?

This sounds like something on your host is setup wrong. Do you have smaller MTU than 1500 by any chance?

> Do you run as root or rootless? Does it work better in a container with `podman run`? Have you tried `--network=host`?
> 
> This sounds like something on your host is setup wrong. Do you have smaller MTU than 1500 by any chance?

I run rootless, I haven't tried `--network host` yet. Also I run podman on my home server where ethernet is connected through WiFi,  MTU is 1500

@sbrivio-rh podman build (buildah) does not support pasta.
This on my TODO to migrate all rootless networking code to c/common then make buildah/podman use the same code.

> @sbrivio-rh podman build (buildah) does not support pasta.

Sorry, yes, I meant if @playXE can try using `podman run` (which I had taken for granted but at a second glance it might not be the case).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 Does podman build support pasta now?
I guess we have pasta support in buildah now, so I am going to close this issue.
